findings.title: CSP allows unsafe-inline for styles enabling CSS injection
findings.description: The Content-Security-Policy in preview-template.html includes style-src 'unsafe-inline' which permits CSS injection. Injected CSS could theoretically exfiltrate data via background-image URL requests or use CSS selectors to detect page content. However, exploitation requires attacker-controlled input flowing into generated styles, pages being served to third parties, and no img-src restriction -- all unlikely in local preview context.
findings.impact: Low. Local preview files, agent-mediated content generation, script-src 'none' blocks primary XSS vector. CSS exfiltration requires additional conditions not present in this architecture.
findings.recommendation: Accepted trade-off for MVP. Consider adding 'img-src self data:;' to CSP to block external URL loading from CSS in a future hardening pass.

findings.title: Node.js command execution for JSON validation
findings.description: Verification step uses 'node -e JSON.parse(...)' to validate tokens.json. JSON.parse is safe against code execution (unlike eval), and the file is agent-generated. Pattern is correct but worth noting for future reference.
findings.location: skills/design-system-init/SKILL.md (Phase 4, referenced in tech-spec.md verification plan)
findings.impact: None. JSON.parse is safe. Agent generates the file content.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Continue using JSON.parse, never switch to eval or require for untrusted JSON.

findings.title: Template placeholders lack auto-escaping
findings.description: {{PAGE_TITLE}}, {{PAGE_STYLES}}, {{PAGE_CONTENT}} are replaced by the agent without HTML entity escaping. Mitigated by Decision 9 filename validation (/^[a-z0-9-]+$/), CSP script-src 'none', and agent-controlled content generation.
findings.impact: None with current mitigations. Defense-in-depth (validation + CSP) covers this.
findings.recommendation: No action required for MVP. Current defense-in-depth is adequate.

findings.title: Read-only skills correctly skip path validation
findings.description: design-review and design-retrospective do not perform filename validation because they either read-only or write to fixed paths. This is correct behavior -- noted for completeness.

findings.title: Subagent file paths from git diff are implicitly trusted
findings.description: The design-review hook in do-task passes git-diff-derived file paths to the subagent without explicit validation. Git-derived paths are trustworthy in local development context.
findings.impact: None. Git diff output is a trusted source within local development.
